title: seq-validate report
description: Stable JSON contract emitted by `seq-validate --json`. This is schema version 1; the `schema_version` field below pins it and any breaking change to the field set bumps it. A single shape covers both a successful validation (`error` null, `sequence` populated) and a harness/parse error (`error` set, `sequence` null).

schema_version.description: Version of this report schema. Pin it; a bump signals a breaking change.

file.description: Input .seq path, exactly as given on the CLI.

error.description: Harness/parse error message, or null on success. When set, `sequence` is null and the process exit code is 2.

sequence.description: Parsed-sequence metadata; null if and only if `error` is set.

name.description: The `Name` definition, or null if the file declared none.

parse_warnings.description: Non-fatal interpreter warnings raised while lowering to the IR.

results.description: Per-check results, in the order checks were run.

id.description: Stable `<category>.<name>` identifier (e.g. "integrity.raster_alignment"). The category prefix groups results.

status.description: Outcome. Only `fail` drives a nonzero exit code; `skip` is a first-class, non-failing result.

measured.description: Observed value (any JSON type), or null if the check measured nothing.

expected.description: Expected value (any JSON type), typically from a spec, or null when there is no expectation.

severity.description: How seriously to treat a non-pass; orthogonal to `status`, does not affect the exit code.
